_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Availability of Laparoscopic surgery and care at tertiary facilities.
clinical_key_pointers: Ureterolithotomy Open. & Lap.: refers to the open or laparoscopic surgical removal of a stone from the ureter. Symptoms: renal colic, pain is typically intermittent/constant, radiation of pain into the ipsilateral groin or testicle varies from severe, sharp/stabbing pain to a dull ache. Urine analysis and urine microscopy is positive for occult blood. Follow up: A CT urogram usually performed 3-6 months postoperatively to rule out a stricture or residual stones, also, Ultrasound can be used to evaluate for silent hydronephrosis. Evaluate for metabolic risk factors, and further stone formation.
additional_information.post_op_care: A CT urogram usually performed 3-6 months postoperatively to rule out a stricture or residual stones, also, Ultrasound can be used to evaluate for silent hydronephrosis. Evaluate for metabolic risk factors, and further stone formation.
